attractions.name: Lake Las Vegas Resort Area
attractions.category: Resort Area, Entertainment, Golf, Shopping, Dining
attractions.description: Lake Las Vegas is a premier 3,600-acre master-planned residential and recreational community centered around a 320-acre man-made freshwater lake. The resort features an old-world Mediterranean theme with Italian-style architecture, offering luxury hotels, championship golf, waterfront dining, and a variety of outdoor water activities.
attractions.hours: The Village complex: Open 24/7 (Business hours vary); Seasons Grocery: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM daily; Reflection Bay Golf: Mon–Thu 10:30 AM – Sunset, Fri–Sun 7:00 AM – Sunset.
attractions.admission: Free entry to the Village; Aqua Park: ~$28; Electric Boat Rentals: ~$100/hr; Golf rates vary by season.
highlights: ['320-acre freshwater lake with 10 miles of scenic shoreline.', 'Mediterranean-style MonteLago Village featuring waterfront shops and restaurants.', 'Championship golf courses designed by Jack Nicklaus, including Reflection Bay.', 'Extensive water sports such as flyboarding, kayaking, and Duffy boat rentals.', 'Luxury accommodations at the Hilton and Westin Lake Las Vegas resorts.', 'Seasonal live entertainment and events on a unique floating stage.']
attractions.tips: Rent an electric boat for an hour to enjoy a full loop of the lake at a relaxing pace. Check the events calendar for live music on the floating stage during summer months. Self-parking is generally complimentary throughout the village area.
attractions.nearby: Lake Mead National Recreation Area, River Mountain Loop Trail, Henderson Bird Viewing Preserve, Cowabunga Bay Water Park, Clark County Wetlands Park.

attractions.name: Reflection Bay Golf Club
attractions.description: Reflection Bay Golf Club is a premier Jack Nicklaus Signature Design course situated on the scenic shores of Lake Las Vegas. The facility features a lavish 32,000-square-foot clubhouse, five holes along the shoreline, and a private white sand beach (Reflection Bay Golf Club, VIP Golf Services).
attractions.hours: Daily: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM (Hours may vary seasonally or by sunset) (Waze, Lake Las Vegas)
attractions.admission: Tee times vary by season and time of day, typically ranging from $150 to $350+ for non-residents; discounted rates are available for Clark County residents with valid ID (Reflection Bay Golf Club).
highlights: ['Jack Nicklaus Signature Design championship layout', '32,000-square-foot clubhouse with full golf shop and dining', 'Five picturesque holes played along the Lake Las Vegas shoreline', 'Home to the High-Performance Golf Institute for elite instruction', 'Access to a private white sand beach and luxury yacht experiences']
attractions.tips: The club is a cashless facility, so be sure to have a credit or debit card for all purchases. Golfers should adhere to the standard golf dress code, and local residents must present a valid Clark County ID to secure discounted green fees (Reflection Bay Golf Club).
attractions.nearby: Montelago Village, Lake Las Vegas Water Sports, Ethel M Chocolates Factory and Cactus Garden, and the Clark County Heritage Museum (Tripadvisor).

It is uniquely known for its water-efficient demonstration gardens that provide educational examples of desert landscaping alongside traditional amenities like lighted sports fields, a splash pad, and a dog park. attractions.name: Black Mountain Golf and Country Club, Review-Journal
attractions.description: Black Mountain Golf and Country Club was a historic 27-hole championship golf facility nestled in the foothills of Henderson, Nevada. Opened in 1957, it was one of the oldest courses in the Las Vegas Valley, featuring a traditional parkland layout with mature trees and stunning mountain vistas. The course ceased operations in November 2018 and is currently being redeveloped into a residential community. Rose Homes, VIP Golf Services
attractions.hours: Historically 6:30 AM - 4:30 PM daily (All week), Tripadvisor
attractions.admission: Historically $26 - $95 depending on residency and time of day (Facility closed in 2018), UnderPar, Albrecht Golf Guide
highlights: '27-hole championship layout comprising the Founders, Horizon, and Desert nines, [Vegas Legal Magazine', "One of Southern Nevada's oldest and most historic golf destinations, established in 1957, UnderPar", 'Traditional parkland design with tree-lined fairways that rewarded accuracy over distance, VIP Golf Services', 'Panoramic views of the Black Mountains and the Mojave Desert landscape, Exploring Las Vegas', 'Comprehensive practice center with a driving range and a 5,000 square foot chipping green, Las Vegas Homes by Leslie', 'Former host of USGA Championship qualifying rounds and the Las Vegas Open, Two Guys Who Golf']
attractions.tips: Historically, accuracy was paramount as errant drives were often punished by the corridors of cypress, olive, and pine trees. The Desert Nine provided a more modern target-golf challenge with elevation changes, while the Founders and Horizon nines offered a classic parkland feel. Visitors enjoyed the relaxed social atmosphere at the clubhouse restaurant, which offered panoramic mountain views. VIP Golf Services, Two Guys Who Golf

attractions.name: Clark County Wetlands Park Nature Center
attractions.description: Clark County Wetlands Park spans 2,900 acres on the eastern edge of the Las Vegas Valley, serving as a lush oasis for wildlife and a natural water purifier for the region. The park features a 210-acre Nature Preserve with diverse ecosystems, interactive exhibits at its LEED gold-rated Nature Center, and miles of scenic trails for hiking and birdwatching (Clark County official site, Nature Center info).
attractions.address: 7050 Wetlands Park Lane, Las Vegas, NV 89122
attractions.hours: Nature Center: Tue–Sun 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM (Closed Monday); Park Trails & Trailheads: Daily, Dawn to Dusk (Clark County official site)
attractions.days_open: Nature Center: Tuesday–Sunday; Park Trails: Daily (Clark County official site)
attractions.admission: Free (Clark County official site)
highlights: '210-acre Nature Preserve with a network of paved and unpaved trails ([Everyday Vegas)', 'Interactive Exhibit Gallery featuring a 12-foot snake, rideable dragonfly, and metal reeds (Nature Center info)', '14-mile Wetlands Loop Trail for ambitious hikers and cyclists (Everyday Vegas)', 'Nature Center facilities including a gift store, auditorium, and the Lizard Lounge picnic room (Clark County official site)', 'Habitat for over 300 species of plants and animals, including roadrunners, turtles, and beavers (Visit Las Vegas)']
attractions.tips: Visitors should bring water, sunscreen, and a hat as temperatures can be high. Note that dogs, bicycles, and skateboards are prohibited within the Nature Preserve area to protect wildlife habitat (Park Brochure PDF). Keep a camera ready for fast-moving roadrunners (Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: Ethel M Botanical Cactus Garden
attractions.description: The Ethel M Botanical Cactus Garden is Nevada's largest and one of the world's most prolific collections of its kind, featuring over 300 species of desert flora across three acres. Established in 1981 alongside the Ethel M Chocolate Factory, the garden showcases a diverse array of cacti, succulents, and desert trees native to the American Southwest, Australia, and South America. It serves as a peaceful, sustainable desert oasis that recycles factory wastewater to hydrate its drought-resistant plants.

attractions.hours: 9am - 7pm (Monday - Sunday), Ethel M Official Website

attractions.tips: Visit during the holiday season (early November to early January) to see the garden transformed with festive lights. It serves as a visual gateway to Henderson's redevelopment, offering residents and visitors an engaging way to explore the area's culture (Airial Travel).

attractions.description: Black Mountain is a landmark peak in the North McCullough Range of the Sloan Canyon National Conservation Area. The trail, also known as BLM 404, offers a strenuous hike with a class-two rock scramble near the summit and rewards hikers with 360-degree panoramic views of the Las Vegas Valley, Red Rock Canyon, and the surrounding desert landscape (Wikipedia), BirdAndHike).

attractions.tips: The trail is best hiked between October and May to avoid extreme desert heat. Hikers should bring at least 3 liters of water, wear sturdy hiking boots for loose rocks, and consider using gloves for the final summit scramble (Traveling Mooses, BirdAndHike).

Visitors can explore a modern exhibit hall featuring a timeline from prehistoric times to the present, as well as a collection of restored historic buildings on 'Heritage Street.' The campus also includes a ghost town, a mining trail, and vintage railroad cars that illustrate the region's social and industrial evolution. Official Site, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Henderson Bird Viewing Preserve
attractions.description: The Henderson Bird Viewing Preserve is a 140-acre desert oasis situated on the eastern edge of the Pacific Migratory Flyway within the city's Water Reclamation Facility. It features nine tranquil ponds and five miles of trails that provide a critical habitat for thousands of migratory waterfowl and resident desert birds. Awarded the title of "Best Place to Birdwatch" in KNPR's Best of the City 2024, it offers a peaceful environment for wildlife observation, education, and nature photography. (City of Henderson)
attractions.hours: Open daily: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM; Summer Hours (June-August): 6:00 AM – Noon. Last entry is 30 minutes before closing. (City of Henderson)

attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to bring water, a hat, and sunscreen due to limited shade in the desert environment. Pets, biking, running, and feeding the wildlife are prohibited to ensure the safety and natural behavior of the birds. It is recommended to arrive early, especially during peak migration seasons or before the summer heat. (City of Henderson)

attractions.name: River Mountains Loop Trail
attractions.description: The River Mountains Loop Trail is a 34-mile non-motorized paved path that surrounds the scenic River Mountains in Southern Nevada. It provides a unique outdoor recreation experience with stunning vistas of Lake Mead, the Mojave Desert, and local wildlife, connecting the communities of Henderson, Boulder City, and the Lake Mead National Recreation Area.

attractions.tips: Always carry more water than you think you'll need, especially during the summer months when desert temperatures can be extreme. attractions.name: Sloan Canyon National Conservation Area
attractions.description: Designated in 2002, Sloan Canyon National Conservation Area preserves 48,438 acres of the Mojave Desert, featuring the southern mountainous skyline of Las Vegas and Henderson. It is home to the renowned Sloan Canyon Petroglyph Site, which contains more than 300 rock art panels and nearly 1,700 designs from the Archaic to historic eras. (Bureau of Land Management)
attractions.hours: October 1 – May 31: Daily 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM; June 1 – September 30: Friday – Sunday 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM (Bureau of Land Management)
attractions.days_open: Daily (October 1 – May 31); Friday – Sunday (June 1 – September 30) (Bureau of Land Management)
attractions.admission: Free (Bureau of Land Management)
highlights: 'Sloan Canyon Petroglyph Site with nearly 1,700 designs ([Bureau of Land Management)', '14,790-acre North McCullough Wilderness area (Bureau of Land Management)', 'Extensive trail system for hiking, mountain biking, and equestrian use (Nevada Trail Finder)', 'Unique volcanic rock formations and lava flows (Bureau of Land Management)', 'Panoramic views of the Las Vegas Valley (Real Journey Travels)']
attractions.tips: Dogs are prohibited on the Petroglyph Trail and in Petroglyph Canyon. Bring at least one gallon of water per person, wear sun protection, and avoid hiking in midday summer heat. Do not touch or take rubbings of the petroglyphs to preserve them. (Bureau of Land Management)

attractions.name: Sloan Canyon Petroglyph Site (Sloan Canyon National Conservation Area)
attractions.description: The Sloan Canyon Petroglyph Site is one of the most significant and important cultural resources in southern Nevada, featuring more than 300 rock writing panels and nearly 1,700 designs. Often called the "Sistine Chapel of Native American rock art," the site preserves artifacts from native cultures dating from the Archaic to historic eras. (Bureau of Land Management, Wikipedia)

attractions.tips: Dogs and service animals are prohibited in Petroglyph Canyon to protect the artifacts. Bring 4-8 quarts of water and wear sturdy shoes, as the trail involves rock scrambling.
